Convert a WordPress WXR XML export into Markdown content

Usage:
  nectar import-wordpress [--on-conflict <skip|overwrite|rename>] [--dry-run] [--json] <file>

Arguments:
  file                Path to a WordPress WXR XML export (Tools → Export in wp-admin produces this)

Options:
  --on-conflict <skip|overwrite|rename>  How to handle existing files when slugs collide: skip (default), overwrite, or rename
  --dry-run           Parse the export and print a summary of what would land (posts, drafts, type/status-filtered items, empty bodies, conflicts) without writing files
  -j, --json          Emit the import summary as JSON on stdout for CI consumption
  -h, --help          Show help for this command

Environment variables:
  Every flag has an env var fallback (CLI flag > env var > config > default).
  Naming: NECTAR_<COMMAND>_<FLAG> (uppercased, dashes become underscores).
  Example: --on-conflict → NECTAR_IMPORT_WORDPRESS_ON_CONFLICT

Repeated flags:
  Scalar string flags use the last value.
  List-style string flags accumulate as comma-separated values in argv order.
  Boolean flags use the last positive or negated spelling.

Examples:
  nectar import-wordpress wordpress.xml
  nectar import-wordpress wordpress.xml --dry-run
  nectar import-wordpress wordpress.xml --on-conflict rename
